expectations.text: Triggers design gate (Phase 0) due to ambiguity
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 (line 60-61) states the design gate is needed when there are '3+ distinct features, external integrations, or ambiguous requirements.' The prompt has all three: payments/credit cards/refunds (3+ features), payment processing (external integration), and 'idk the details yet' (ambiguous requirements). No flag override (--skip-design) is present, so the gate triggers.

expectations.text: Asks clarifying questions one at a time (not a questionnaire dump)
expectations.evidence: Phase 0, line 67 explicitly instructs: 'Ask the user one clarifying question at a time (do not dump a list)'. This directly addresses the assertion's requirement of avoiding a questionnaire dump.

expectations.text: Does NOT call init-state.ts or start Phase 3 before design approval
expectations.evidence: The phases are strictly sequential (Phase 0 -> 1 -> 2 -> 3). Phase 0 must complete ('Synthesize a design summary once alignment is reached') before Phase 1 runs. Phase 3 (init-state.ts) depends on Phase 2's work units output, and Phase 2 depends on Phase 0's design summary. There is no mechanism to skip ahead to Phase 3 when Phase 0 is triggered.
Phase 0, line 68 explicitly instructs: 'Propose trade-offs where the spec is ambiguous'. The payment spec is highly ambiguous ('idk the details yet'), so this instruction directly applies.

expectations.text: Waits for explicit user confirmation before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Multiple confirmation gates exist: Phase 0 uses iterative questioning ('one clarifying question at a time') requiring user responses, synthesizing only 'once alignment is reached' (line 69). Phase 2 line 101 says 'Present the work plan to the user and wait for confirmation'. Phase 4 line 128 says 'Once the user confirms the plan, execution is fully autonomous.' The pipeline cannot proceed without user interaction.

expectations.text: Design summary feeds into work decomposition (Phase 2)
expectations.evidence: Three explicit structural connections: (1) Phase 0 line 69: 'Store the design summary for later phases'. (2) Phase 2 line 89: 'Analyze the spec and design summary (if any)'. (3) Phase 3 line 119: 'Pass --design-summary "{summary}" if Phase 0 ran'. The design summary is stored, consumed by decomposition, and passed to state initialization.
